headline: Apple’s latest MacBook Air is $200 off in both sizes for Memorial Day
summary: The latest MacBook Air models are currently $200 off for Memorial Day. This deal applies to both 13-inch and 15-inch sizes. It's a great opportunity to get a powerful M5 chip laptop at a discount.

headline: Hackers are learning to exploit chatbot ‘personalities’
summary: Cybercriminals are developing new methods to exploit AI chatbot "personalities." This represents an evolution from simpler hacks of early AI models. The Stepback newsletter details these emerging threats in the tech world.

headline: Why Nuro thinks being a robotaxi ‘second mover’ gives it an advantage
summary: Nuro believes being a "second mover" in the robotaxi market offers unique benefits. Waymo currently dominates the self-driving car industry. Nuro aims to differentiate itself despite the strong competition from other firms.

headline: Record Club is trying to be Letterboxd for music nerds
summary: Record Club is a new platform aiming to be the Letterboxd or Goodreads for music lovers. It offers a clean interface for cataloging listening habits and connecting with fans. The app seeks to improve on existing, more crowded music review sites.

headline: How to Read The Sun Eater Books and Novellas in Order
summary: A guide details the chronological reading order for The Sun Eater book series. It includes both the main novels and supplemental novellas. This resource helps fans navigate the complex sci-fi saga.

headline: Black Flag’s Edward Kenway Is the Best Assassin Because He Doesn’t Care for the Creed
summary: Edward Kenway from Assassin's Creed Black Flag is argued to be the series' best protagonist. His disregard for the Assassin's Creed lore sets him apart. This perspective offers a unique take on the popular game character.

headline: Hands Over, a Horror Party Game, Announced for PC
summary: "Hands Over," a new multiplayer horror party game, has been announced for PC. It twists classic childhood mini-games with a deadly horror theme. The game is currently in development by Artdock.

headline: How X-Men ’97 and Devil May Cry’s Studio Mir Became a Backbone of American Animation
summary: Korean Studio Mir has become a key contributor to American animation. They are known for hits like "The Legend of Korra," "X-Men '97," and "Devil May Cry." The studio's work highlights its significant impact on Western animated productions.

headline: Girl raped by boys spared jail tells BBC judge's decision was like 'rock in my face'
summary: A girl who was raped expressed profound shock over the judge's decision to spare the perpetrators jail time. She described the verdict as a "rock in my face" in an interview with the BBC. Sir Keir Starmer has called for a review of the "appalling case."

headline: Large-scale Russian attack on Ukraine leaves four dead and dozens injured
summary: A major Russian missile attack has resulted in four deaths and dozens of injuries in Ukraine. Russia confirmed the use of its Oreshnik missile, which travels over 10 times the speed of sound. The attack caused widespread devastation and casualties.

headline: 'Six eggs used to be £1' - why everyday essentials cost so much more now
summary: The price of everyday essentials, like eggs, has significantly increased since 2022. The article explores the reasons behind the rising cost of goods. It investigates whether any profiteering is contributing to the price hikes.

headline: It's like the Olympics, except steroids are allowed
summary: The first-ever "Enhanced Games" are set to take place in Las Vegas this weekend. This controversial event allows the use of performance-enhancing drugs. It features prominent names, significant money, and has sparked much debate.
